findings.title: Unrestricted first-time referral initialization lets an attacker seize reward routing and brick reward-dependent share updates

best_agent_match.title: Unrestricted Rescue Functions Allow Draining of Protocol Assets

findings.title: Anyone can front-run a user’s first claim and permanently bind attacker-controlled referrers

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can front-run a user's first claim and permanently assign attacker-controlled referrers

findings.title: Dust supply causes the automatic fee path to execute a zero-input swap and can freeze transfers/sells

best_agent_match.title: Dust total supply makes the auto-fee path call a zero-amount swap and can freeze transfers

findings.title: Repeated failed reward swaps can wrap slippage math and strand protocol DAI fees

best_agent_match.title: Repeated failed reward swaps can underflow slippage math and strand DAI fees

rejected_candidates.title: Bond mints redeemable index tokens before collecting the full basket, enabling malicious-asset reentrancy
rejected_candidates.reason: Reentrancy is theoretically possible, but no concrete path was shown to extract net value or leave the index undercollateralized: if the outer `bond()` later fails the whole transaction reverts, and if it succeeds the full basket is still collected.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted Rescue Functions Allow Draining of Protocol Assets
rejected_candidates.reason: `rescueERC20()` and `rescueETH()` send funds to `Ownable(address(V3_TWAP_UTILS)).owner()`, not to the caller. Anyone can trigger an admin rescue, but that is not an attacker-controlled drain.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted Staking Pool Token Transfers Bypass Stake Restrictions
rejected_candidates.reason: The restriction only limits who may be minted staking receipts in `stake()`. Transferring receipt tokens later does not mint extra stake or create a realistic protocol-level loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in WeightedIndex Constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a deployment-time misconfiguration only, not an exploitable vulnerability in a live protocol.

rejected_candidates.title: Uniswap V2 Price Manipulation via Flash Loans
rejected_candidates.reason: The reserve-derived price helpers are only exposed through view functions; `bond()` and `debond()` do not use these prices for settlement.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Callback Without Reentrancy Protection
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete unsafe state dependency was identified. The callback must restore the borrowed token balance before returning, and the report did not show a profitable reentrant path.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Slippage Protection on Token Swap
rejected_candidates.reason: This is ordinary slippage/MEV exposure around an allowed swap, not a distinct code bug with a demonstrated protocol-breaking path.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation of Token Decimals in WeightedIndex
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on deploying an index with malicious or highly non-standard token metadata and does not present a realistic permissionless exploit against an already deployed index.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value in ETH Transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract does check the result of the ETH send and reverts on failure via `require(_sent, 'SENT')`.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Integer Overflow in Price Calculations
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited math is in view-only pricing helpers, not in settlement logic, and no realistic overflow exploit path was demonstrated for standard reserve and decimal ranges.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero-Address Validation in Constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a deployment/configuration risk rather than a live, permissionless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on StakingPoolToken Admin Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: If restrictions are disabled at deployment, the inability to later enable them is a governance/design limitation, not a realistic protocol-harm vulnerability.
